results.title: Facilities Engineer II - MyPetroCareer
highlights:
Facilities Engineer II - MyPetroCareer

News Ticker

- [ June 2, 2026 ] NNPC eyes AI-driven growth through strategic SAP technology partnership News
- [ June 1, 2026 ] Global LNG market faces fresh pressure as summer heat threatens supply crunch News
- [ June 1, 2026 ] FG commissions high-capacity CNG station in Abuja News
- [ June 1, 2026 ] Gas investment surges as oil spending declines globally News
- [ May 31, 2026 ] Oil revenue misses Q3 target by ₦7.88tn amid fiscal strain News

Login to Save this Job

EOG Resources

 

This position is responsible for the detailed engineering design and overall project management of upstream facilities projects in the Utica Basin. Role is responsible for overall infrastructure management of assigned area and for interfacing with reservoir, planning, drilling, completions, water resources, production, supply chain, accounting, land, legal, regulatory, marketing, environmental, and safety teammates. Ideal candidate will be knowledgeable in most concepts of onshore production facilities, with a strong emphasis on safety, regulatory, and environmental practices. Position will be located in EOG’s New Albany Ohio office. Periodic travel to field locations will be required. Position will be filled at a level commensurate with experience.

- Function as infrastructure planning and project manager for assigned area.
- Develop and maintain hydraulic models for gas, oil, and produced water systems. Manage detailed pipeline design, along with interfacing with land and regulatory teams to acquire ROW and permits.
- Manage the design, installation, expansion, and commissioning of central production facilities.
- Serve as project manager for oil, gas, and produced water pipeline projects.
- Create process simulation models for production facilities.
- Lead development of equipment specifications.
- Forecast equipment, valves, fittings, and pipe needs. Work in conjunction with supply chain management team to meet procurement requirements.
- Provide facilities engineering and maintenance support for production operations.
- Develop AFE’s for area projects and oversee cost tracking and forecasting efforts.
- Promote process optimization, cost reduction, and emission reduction initiatives.
- Participate in mechanical integrity program
- Serve as a safety leader within the division organization.

Job Description

Job Requirements

 

- Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Petroleum, or Electrical Engineering with five or more years of relevant industry experience.
- Working knowledge of upstream or midstream production facilities, process equipment design and operation and field construction activities.
- Awareness of industry codes and design standards; including environmental, regulatory, and safety regulations.
- Candidate must have strong communication, interpersonal and technical skills, with the proven ability to build relationships and work in a multi-disciplined team. Must have the ability to work in a highly innovative, fast-paced environment with frequently changing priorities.

EOG Resources is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including Veterans and Disabled.

To apply for this job please visit jobs.smartsearchonline.com.

results.title: Jobs at EOG Resources - Discover How to Apply for Job
highlights:
Jobs at EOG Resources - Discover How to Apply for Job
[...]
For those considering energy sector careers, learning about opportunities at EOG Resources, the available job categories, and the application journey could be quite valuable.
[...]
## What Roles Does EOG Resources Offer?
[...]
The energy sector includes far more than fieldwork or engineering. EOG’s workforce is diverse—spanning field operations, geosciences, environmental management, IT, finance, logistics, and more. Most jobs fall into a few categories, each with distinct requirements and daily tasks.
[...]
Candidates interested in business
[...]
look into accounting,
[...]
, HR, legal,
[...]
## How Does the Application Process at EOG Resources Work?
[...]
Most roles at EOG Resources are advertised on the official EOG careers website. Sometimes, roles also appear on job boards like Indeed or LinkedIn. Locating open positions usually isn’t too complicated, though competition can be high.
[...]
### Steps to Apply Online
[...]
- Finance & Career Benefits
- Visit the company’s official Careers page and use filters to find open jobs that match your skills. Sorting by location or job family is usually possible.
- Review each listing’s requirements carefully. If uncertain about fit, EOG typically prefers applicants who closely meet the job qualifications. A tailored resume often performs better than a generic one.
[...]
- Complete the online application form provided on EOG’s site. Be prepared to upload a resume and, sometimes, a cover letter or references. Some fields (like safety certifications) should be filled in detail, if relevant.
[...]
- After submission, applicants may receive a confirmation email. Silence is not uncommon during initial screenings but could also mean the process is ongoing.
[...]
Depending on the role, EOG’s selection process could involve phone screenings, online assessments, and several rounds of interviews. Technical jobs might involve case studies or technical questions, while business roles may focus on teamwork and situational challenges.
[...]
## Qualifications and Tips for EOG Applications
[...]
Most field and technical jobs require a high school diploma or diploma in a technical field. Engineering, geosciences, or corporate positions typically need a university degree—sometimes advanced. Certain jobs (like those in safety or management) may ask for specific certifications or licenses.
[...]
- Detail all industry certifications —such as HSE or welding credentials—clearly in your application.
- Highlight both solo projects and teamwork; EOG often values self-motivated individuals who also collaborate well.
- Mention experience with relevant industry software (examples: AutoCAD, SAP, or drilling simulation tools) when required.
- Adding brief context about how you handled unexpected events—like safety incidents or shifting priorities—might set your application apart, provided it’s genuine.
[...]
## Table: Typical EOG Resources Job Categories and Sample Requirements
[...]
| Job Family | Common Qualifications | Sample Duties |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Field Operations | HS diploma, safety certs | Site maintenance, equipment operation |
| Engineering | BS engineering, field exp. | Project design, optimization |
| Geosciences | Geology degree, data analysis | Resource assessment, mapping |
| Business/Admin | Degree or exp., office skills | Payroll, scheduling, compliance |
[...]
Exploring open jobs at EOG Resources, while preparing a tailored and thoughtful application, is usually the strongest first step. And if you’re still comparing options, browsing role requirements and reading employee experiences may provide even more clarity on where you’d like your career to head.

results.title: Senior AI and ML Scientists (multiple positions) - Aker BP
highlights:
Senior AI and ML Scientists (multiple positions) - Aker BP
[...]
# Senior AI and ML Scientists (multiple positions)
[...]
Join our established AI team, bringing scientific depth and rigor to drive innovation across diverse specializations, from agentic AI to industrial vision and signal machine learning, and ensure robust, uncertainty-aware production systems.
[...]
We are seeking Senior AI and ML Scientists across several critical specialisations to bring scientific depth to our established AI organization. These roles are pivotal in raising evaluation rigor, mentoring across the team, and taking ownership of the specific modalities our industrial use cases demand.
[...]
You will apply scientific rigor across all stages of AI delivery, from initial problem framing through to evaluation design. This involves technical deep dives within your chosen specialization, contributing to our collective knowledge, and raising the bar across the team. A key aspect is mentoring data scientists and engineers, and actively moving research into production through collaboration with MLOps, architecture, and pod engineers. You will also contribute to establishing uncertainty quantification standards, understanding how prediction confidence and calibration affect safe automation thresholds, and promoting software engineering discipline in scientific work, including version-controlled experiments, peer-reviewed code, and production-ready implementations.
[...]
Track 1: Principal AI / ML Scientist
[...]
Provide scientific leadership across the full team. Set the evaluation bar, mentor across specialisations, shape research direction, and represent the AI function in architectural and strategic decisions. A high-leverage senior hire.
[...]
PhD in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Statistics, or related field — or equivalent demonstrated depth.
[...]
Track record of moving research into production, not just publishing.
[...]
Deep working knowledge of uncertainty quantification, calibration, and out-of-distribution behaviour in autonomous AI.
[...]
Track 2: Senior ML Engineer / Applied Scientist — Language and Agents
[...]
Bring deep expertise in LLM-based systems, agentic reasoning, RAG quality, evaluation frameworks for probabilistic outputs, and multi-agent coordination. Translate uncertainty in LLM outputs into concrete risk thresholds and operational guardrails.
[...]
Hands-on production experience shipping ML or LLM-based applications, ideally including agentic patterns.
[...]
Demonstrated work on evaluation methodology for probabilistic and non-deterministic outputs.
[...]
Hands-on ML and AI architecture experience including evaluation of quality performance.
[...]
Experience with calibrated uncertainty estimation in production.
[...]
Track 3: Senior Signal Processing / Acoustic ML Engineer
[...]
Specialist depth in acoustic signal processing and related machine-learning approaches. Relevant across subsurface, operations, and equipment monitoring use cases.
[...]
Track 4: Senior Computer Vision Engineer / Scientist
[...]
PhD in Computer Vision
[...]
Our ambition is clear: to become the world’s first AI‑native exploration and production company.
[...]
We're hiring to accelerate our journey towards becoming the first AI-native E&P company.
[...]
We didn't discover AI last year. For a decade, we've been building the data foundation and interoperable ecosystem that now lets us scale AI across some of the most complex operations in our industry.
[...]
Our ambition is simple to state and hard to deliver: a 3x productivity improvement. Getting there demands more than incremental gains, it requires a fundamental redesign of how work gets done. You get there by redesigning how decisions are made, how work is planned, and how assets are run, with AI agents as native participants in the operating model. And that's why we're looking for ambitious, driven AI professionals who want to help rewrite how a large industrial company operates.

results.title: AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy
highlights:
AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy

Skip to
[...]
AI Machine Learning Engineer at Occidental Petroleum | Jeremy
[...]
Occidental Petroleum
[...]
# AI Machine Learning Engineer
3 days agoHouston, TexasMid-LevelSeniorML / AI
[...]
LLMAIDeep LearningRelational DatabasesCloudModel TrainingData EngineeringCausal Inference
[...]
Research and build AI/ML systems for oil and gas applications including subsurface characterization and reservoir engineering at Occidental Petroleum's AI Center of Excellence in Houston.
[...]
## Full Job Description
[...]
We are looking for an experienced and innovative individual contributor to fill the position of **AI Machine Learning Engineer **within our **AI Center of Excellence** group based in **Houston, TX. **The incumbent will research, build, and design artificial intelligence systems to automate predictive models, and design machine learning systems, models and schemes. 
**Essential Job Responsibilities**
[...]
* Work with Subject Matter Experts to design appropriate machine learning model types and architectures for particular applications ranging from subsurface characterization, reservoir engineering, production, and facilities etc
* Tune algorithm hyper-parameters and perform model training and validation
* Create workflows to optimize performance for computationally heavy workloads
* Collaborate with Machine Learning Operation team to deploy machine learning solutions to the cloud
[...]
**Qualifications**
[...]
* Bachelor's degree in computer science, mathematics, physics, engineering or related field required
* Master's degree is highly preferred. Equivalent combination of advanced education and relevant experience will be considered.
* A minimum of 5 years of experience and deep knowledge in orchestration methods and tools to optimally use computational infrastructure, and machine learning methods and algorithms
* Strong Python or R programming skills required
* Experience developing, architecting, and running ML or deep learning workload in the Cloud.
* Ability to express the intuition behind basic ML algorithms
* Experience performing basic hyper parameter optimization
* Ability to follow model training, deployment, and operational best practices
* Experience with Docker for containerization and Kubernetes for orchestration
* Knowledge of Terraform and Ansible for automating infrastructure management
* Knowledge of YAML for automating model and application code deployment
* Understanding of data engineering principles and databases (relational, NoSQL)
* Strong critical thinking and communication skills
**Additional Desired Qualifications:**
[...]
* AWS certification is highly preferred, with a strong preference for the AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty credential.
* Experience with Bayesian statistical modeling, including Bayesian inference, Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), and causal inference.
* Hands‑on experience in geospatial data analytics using Esri products, with a focus on ArcGIS Enterprise.
* Experience with LLM-powered applications such as chatbots, question-answering systems, document processing and agentic workflow design.

results.title: AI Solutions Engineer - Aker BP
highlights:
AI Solutions Engineer - Aker BP
[...]
# AI Solutions Engineer
[...]
Are you an AI Solutions Engineer who thrives on translating complex business problems into tangible, production-ready AI solutions within a dynamic, cross-functional environment? Join us and anchor one of our AI delivery pods, making a real impact on our journey to become the first AI-native E&P company.
[...]
Strong technical hands-on background in AI/ML engineering, data engineering, or AI solutions delivery.
[...]
Proven ability to lead and deliver production-ready AI systems, with a strong track record of shipping LLM-based or agentic solutions.
[...]
Deep understanding of probabilistic AI outputs, operational risk, and designing safeguards for agentic workflows.
[...]
About the role
[...]
Drive AI Innovation in the Energy Sector.
[...]
We are building seven cross-functional AI delivery pods aligned to business domains. Each pod needs a technical anchor – someone who can lead end-to-end AI use-case delivery while partnering closely with business domain experts, data engineering, and central AI functions.
[...]
As an AI Solution Engineer, you are the cross-functional glue. You translate business problems into AI solutions, shape technical approach, coordinate with architecture and platform teams, develop and test AI solutions, and make sure what ships actually works in production and adds measurable value.
[...]
Aker BP is building the foundation for agentic AI at scale. We are doing this as one operating model; strategy, architecture, science, data, safety, and delivery designed to work together, not as separate teams. This role is part of that build.
[...]
We are moving AI into the core of industrial operations, work that spans physical assets, regulated environments, and decisions that affect production, safety, and the broader energy system. We work cross-disciplinarily, with a focus on shipping into production rather than running experiments indefinitely.
[...]
Key responsibilities
[...]
AI solution designCollaborate closely with domain specialists to translate business needs into implementable AI solutions.
[...]
Impact-driven prioritisationDrive use-case prioritisation based on data readiness, technical feasibility, and business impact.
[...]
AI delivery and developmentLead the end-to-end technical delivery of AI and agentic AI use cases within your assigned pod.
[...]
Cross-functional coordinationCoordinate with central architecture, MLOps, security, and data governance teams to ensure compliance with enterprise standards.
[...]
Own quality and productionOwn evaluation setup, quality bars, and production readiness, including CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and code-review standards.
[...]
Uncertainty handlingDesign solutions with explicit handling of model uncertainty, defining confidence thresholds, fallback behaviours, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.
[...]
AI-first mindsetWe are an AI-first company, committed to leading our industry in AI adoption and unlocking its transformative power.
[...]
Our ambition is clear: to become the world’s first AI‑native exploration and production company.
[...]
We're hiring to accelerate our journey towards becoming the first AI-native E&P company.
[...]
We didn't discover AI last year. For a decade, we've been building the data foundation and interoperable ecosystem that now lets us scale AI across some of the most complex operations in our industry.
[...]
Our ambition is simple to state and hard to deliver: a 3x productivity improvement. Getting there demands more than incremental gains, it requires a fundamental redesign of how work gets done. You get there by redesigning how decisions are made, how work is planned, and how assets are run, with AI agents as native participants in the operating model.
[...]
And that's why we're looking for ambitious, driven AI professionals who want to help rewrite how a large industrial company operates.

results.title: Digital Technology Experienced Professional – Data Science - MyPetroCareer
highlights:
Digital Technology Experienced Professional – Data Science - MyPetroCareer
[...]
Job Description – Digital Technology Experienced Professional – Data Science
[...]
Baker Hughes stands as a leading global energy-technology company, delivering innovative solutions that make energy safer, cleaner, and more efficient across the entire value chain. Operating in more than 120 countries, the company blends deep industrial expertise with advanced engineering and digital technologies to support energy production, transformation, and decarbonization. With over a century of heritage, Baker Hughes partners with customers in oil and gas, industrial sectors, and emerging energy markets to accelerate progress toward a more sustainable energy future
[...]
As an AI Engineer, you will contribute to the design, development, and deployment of production-grade AI applications, with a strong focus on Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs). You will work closely with experienced AI engineers, data engineers, and specialist product teams to prototype, test, and scale AI-driven capabilities that deliver measurable business value.
[...]
This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, machine learning, and applied AI, and is ideal for an engineer who is excited about building reliable, responsible, and high-quality AI systems rather than standalone models.
[...]
As an AI Engineer, you will be responsible for:
[...]
Assist in developing applications using LLMs and GenAI Tools
[...]
Collaborate with our API Program to ensure quality
[...]
Create, fine tune, and adapt pre-trained models
[...]
Monitor outputs for quality, bias, security, and continued relevance
[...]
Propose and document experiments, prompts, and system designs
[...]
To be successful in this role you will:
[...]
Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related discipline, or equivalent practical experience – preferably with a focus on Data and AI
[...]
Be proficient in Python and SQL, with experience working in modern data and analytics environments
[...]
Have a solid understanding of machine learning and natural language processing (NLP) concepts
[...]
Demonstrate hands-on interest or experience with Generative AI and LLM-based
[...]
Have working knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure or GCP)
[...]
Be comfortable collaborating across disciplines, including engineering, product, and architecture teams
[...]
To apply for this job please visit careers.bakerhughes.com.

EOG Resources

 

Provide Human Resources support to assigned division’s office managers and staff, including field operations. Proactively develop strong business partnership with managers at all levels. Consult with managers in the resolution of employee relations issues and conduct or participate in HR-related investigations. Coach employees and managers, and coordinate training solutions when needed. Coordinate the selection and recruitment of new employees. Counsel with client management and employees on company policies, benefits, staffing, employee relations and compensation issues and programs. Prepare compensation cycle data for senior management review and approval. Assist with special projects and develop and analyze statistical reports as necessary. Prepare, facilitate, and conduct training on a variety of HR initiatives as needed.

Job Description

Job Requirements

 

- High school diploma or GED is required. Bachelor’s degree in Human Resource Management or related business field is preferred.
- SHRM or HRCI certification is a plus.
- Minimum 3 years Human Resources experience.
- Oil and Gas industry experience is preferred.
- Excellent knowledge of employment laws is required.
- Must be results-oriented and have good organizational skills, as well as ability to manage competing priorities.
- Effective critical-thinking and conflict resolution techniques to problem-solve.
- Must be able to communicate effectively and have strong interpersonal skills.
- Must be able to establish relationships and maintain rapport with client groups.
- Must have strong computer skills, including proficiency in MS Office applications.
- Ability to effectively work in an autonomous environment, as well as in a team with other Human Resources staff.
- Must maintain confidentiality in daily operations.
- Approximately 20-40% day trip travel to field offices.
- Support 24/7 operations, requiring occasional need to be accessible outside of standard office work hours.

Position will be filled at a level commensurate with experience.

EOG Resources is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including Veterans and Disabled

results.title: Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria, working closely with domain experts.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Architect and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, ensuring robustness, efficiency, and reproducibility across training and evaluation workflows.
[...]
As a Principal ML Scientist:
[...]
You help define the long-term technical roadmap for scientific reasoning models, identifying new opportunities and setting priorities across initiatives.
[...]
You architect new initiatives
[...]
data modalities, guiding the technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.
[...]
You serve as a key technical authority for leadership, influencing how Genentech leverages generative AI to solve high-stakes problems in the therapeutic pipeline.
[...]
For Senior (SE6): 0 – 2+ years of industry or post-doc experience with a focus on deep learning.
[...]
For Principal (SE7): 5+ years of industry or post-PhD experience with a demonstrated track record of technical leadership and project ownership.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Extensive experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including approaches to improve domain understanding, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Engineering: Strong software engineering skills and experience designing and operating large-scale or high-performance machine learning systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data) is highly valued but not required.
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary range for this position, based on the primary location of San Francisco, is $167,400 – 310,80
[...]
of hiring range for the Senior Scientist, and $194,000 – 360,400 for the Principal Scientist. For the primary of location of New York City, $160,100 – 297,300 for the Senior Scientist, and $185,600 – 344,600 for the Principal Scientist. Actual pay will be determined based on experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors permitted by law. A discretionary annual bonus may be available based on individual and Company performance. This position also qualifies for the benefits detailed at the link provided below.

results.title: Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
highlights:
Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
[...]
# Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery
[...]
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date May 15, 2026
[...]
## Job Details
[...]
We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.
[...]
The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.
[...]
As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
Design, build, and optimize RAG pipelines over internal publications, study reports, electronic lab notebooks, and other scientific documents
[...]
Build hybrid retrieval systems combining vector search with structured metadata, knowledge graphs, and ontology-aware filtering
[...]
Build and optimize text-to-SQL systems over Lilly’s databases, enabling scientists to query gene expression, proteomics, pathway, and variant data through natural language
[...]
Develop schema documentation, semantic annotations, and gold-standard question/SQL pairs that bridge how scientists think about data and how it is stored
[...]
Implement multi-step reasoning approaches (chain-of-thought, self-correction, Reflexion loops) to improve accuracy on complex scientific queries
[...]
Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses
[...]
Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases
[...]
Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs
[...]
PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 0-3+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems; MS in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 5+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems

results.title: Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II | Wfhverse
highlights:
Generative Data Scientist II

Remote, USA

Posted May 31, 2026

Full-time

 Description Position Summary Details We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS. Requirements Essential Duties & Responsibilities The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following: End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach (prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy. Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference. Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation. Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails. Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration). MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies. Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs. Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs. Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities) Education: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s degree preferred. Experience: 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems. MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience. Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals. Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents. Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA). Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services). Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale. Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses. Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems. Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills. Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM. Healthcare experience: familiarity with HIPAA, medical data handling, or working in health tech. Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana). Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral). Other: Tech Stack Languages: Python, SQL DL/LLM: PyTorch, Tensorflow, Hugging Face, PEFT/TRL, vLLM Data: Snowflake, Postgres Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda) MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B Supervisory Responsibilities This role does not have any direct reports and is a single contributor role. Working Environment and Travel Requirements Work is typically in a normal office administrative environment involving minimal exposure to physical risks. Position requires little to moderate physical activity. Mostly sedentary work exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally or a negligible amount of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects. Work involves sitting most of the time, but may involve walking or standing for brief

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:

- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.

- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability

- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.

- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments

- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]

results.title: EOG: Future Cash Flow Will Reflect Prolonged Iran Conflict Oil Tailwinds
highlights:
Analysts have nudged their consolidated price target for EOG Resources higher to $160, citing revised oil price decks tied to the Iran conflict, expectations for tighter global crude supply and refining markets, and updated assumptions for margins and future P
[...]
- EOG doubled its share repurchase authorization to US$20b at the 2026 Annual Meeting, after previously buying back about 59.4 million shares for roughly US$7.1b, and reported total shareholder returns of US$4.7b through free cash flow and dividends in the latest period. (Source: EOG 2026 Annual Meeting coverage, buyback plan update)
[...]
- The company reported Q1 2026 earnings per share of US$3.41 on revenues of US$6.92b, with about US$1.5b of free cash flow and net income around US$2b, supported by crude oil, natural gas, and NGL production across the Delaware Basin, Utica, and Eagle Ford. (Source: Q1 2026 results coverage, operating results announcement)
[...]
- Recent fund letters highlighted EOG as a key contributor to portfolio performance during the Q1 2026 oil price shock related to the Iran conflict, citing the company’s low cost profile and reserve base, while some analysts pointed out that upside in the stock could be limited relative to other opportunities. (Source: Q1 2026 fund commentary)
